# Session Format

Saved chat sessions are stored as JSON files in `.chat/`. This page documents the schema.

## File Naming

```
{timestamp}-{slugified-title}.json
```

Examples:
- `2026-04-12T14-30-fix-auth-bug.json`
- `2026-04-12T14-30-fix-auth-bug-part1.json` (split session)

## JSON Schema

```json
{
  "version": 1,
  "id": "<uuid>",
  "title": "<auto-generated or user-provided>",
  "savedAt": "<ISO 8601 timestamp>",
  "git": {
    "branch": "<current branch name>",
    "commit": "<HEAD commit SHA>",
    "dirty": true
  },
  "vscodeVersion": "<version string>",
  "totalTurns": 12,
  "part": null,
  "totalParts": null,
  "previousPartFile": null,
  "nextPartFile": null,
  "turns": [
    {
      "type": "request",
      "participant": "copilot",
      "prompt": "Fix the authentication bug in login.ts",
      "references": ["src/login.ts"],
      "timestamp": "2026-04-12T14:30:00Z"
    },
    {
      "type": "response",
      "participant": "copilot",
      "content": "I'll fix the authentication bug...",
      "toolCalls": [
        {
          "name": "read_file",
          "summary": "Read src/login.ts",
          "output": "..."
        }
      ],
      "timestamp": "2026-04-12T14:30:05Z"
    }
  ],
  "markdownSummary": "# Chat: Fix Auth Bug\n\n## User\nFix the authentication bug...\n\n## Copilot\nI'll fix the authentication bug..."
}
```

## Field Definitions

### Top-Level Fields

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `version` | `number` | Schema version. Currently `1`. |
| `id` | `string` | UUID for the session |
| `title` | `string` | Auto-generated from first user prompt, or user-provided |
| `savedAt` | `string` | ISO 8601 timestamp of when the session was saved |
| `git` | `object` | Git context at save time |
| `vscodeVersion` | `string` | VS Code version string |
| `totalTurns` | `number` | Total number of turns (across all parts if split) |
| `part` | `number \| null` | Part number if split, else `null` |
| `totalParts` | `number \| null` | Total parts if split, else `null` |
| `previousPartFile` | `string \| null` | Filename of previous part, else `null` |
| `nextPartFile` | `string \| null` | Filename of next part, else `null` |
| `turns` | `array` | Ordered list of conversation turns |
| `markdownSummary` | `string` | Rendered markdown summary for human review |

### Git Object

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `branch` | `string` | Current branch name |
| `commit` | `string` | HEAD commit SHA |
| `dirty` | `boolean` | Whether the working tree has uncommitted changes |

### Turn Object

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `type` | `"request" \| "response"` | Whether this is a user request or assistant response |
| `participant` | `string` | Which chat participant handled this turn |
| `prompt` | `string` | User's prompt text (request turns only) |
| `content` | `string` | Assistant's response text (response turns only) |
| `references` | `string[]` | File references attached to the request |
| `toolCalls` | `array` | Tool invocations made during the response |
| `timestamp` | `string` | ISO 8601 timestamp |

### Tool Call Object

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `name` | `string` | Tool name (e.g., `read_file`, `run_in_terminal`) |
| `summary` | `string` | Brief description of what the tool call did |
| `output` | `string` | Tool output (may be `"[output stripped — N chars]"` if `stripToolOutput` is enabled) |

## TypeScript Types

Defined in `src/types.ts`:

```typescript
interface ChatSession {
  version: number;
  id: string;
  title: string;
  savedAt: string;
  git: GitContext;
  vscodeVersion: string;
  totalTurns: number;
  part: number | null;
  totalParts: number | null;
  previousPartFile: string | null;
  nextPartFile: string | null;
  turns: Turn[];
  markdownSummary: string;
}

interface GitContext {
  branch: string;
  commit: string;
  dirty: boolean;
}

type Turn = RequestTurn | ResponseTurn;

interface RequestTurn {
  type: 'request';
  participant: string;
  prompt: string;
  references: string[];
  timestamp: string;
}

interface ResponseTurn {
  type: 'response';
  participant: string;
  content: string;
  toolCalls: ToolCall[];
  timestamp: string;
}

interface ToolCall {
  name: string;
  summary: string;
  output: string;
}
```

## Split Sessions

When a session exceeds `save.maxFileSize`:
- Turns are distributed across multiple part files
- Each part carries the same top-level metadata (`id`, `title`, `git`, etc.)
- `part`, `totalParts`, `previousPartFile`, `nextPartFile` fields link the chain
- The Resume System reassembles all parts before injecting context
